title: SenderIdOrSelf: bespoke serde with empty-string rejection is correct
description: SenderIdOrSelf has hand-written Serialize/Deserialize implementations rather than deriving them. The deserializer explicitly rejects an empty string with a structured error ('sender_id must not be empty'). A future contributor might suggest replacing this with #[serde(rename = "self")] on one variant and letting derive handle the rest, but that does not work: serde's derive cannot disambiguate a catch-all string variant from a named string variant on the same enum. The manual impl is also the only place where the empty-string invariant is enforced — without it, a malformed server response would silently produce a Contact("") that later code would treat as a valid contact id. The Display impl mirrors the serializer exactly, which is also load-bearing for logging and UI. This complexity is justified and must not be replaced with a derive.
